test.command: echo 'Tests cannot run on Mac - write tests for CI only'
test.notes: Test suite does not run on macOS per services.yaml and AGENTS.md. Tests are written for CI (Linux). No new test failures introduced (pre-existing macOS constraint).

typecheck.notes: 1778 mypy errors found, identical to pre-feature baseline (verified by stashing changes and re-running). All errors are pre-existing. No new type errors introduced by v2-pool-index changes.

lint.notes: 122 ruff errors found, identical to pre-feature baseline (verified by stashing changes and re-running). All errors are pre-existing. No new lint violations introduced.

blockingIssues.description: test_new_pool_added_to_index_incrementally will always time out: the test comment says 'we patch the sleep to speed up the test' but no mocking occurs. The background task sleeps 30 seconds before processing new pools; the test only polls for 5 seconds (50 × 0.1s). Fix: mock asyncio.sleep in the background task, or restructure the test to directly inject a new pool into pools_obj._objects and trigger index update without waiting for the poll loop.

Fallback pools from the 'node can't look back far enough' branch are silently absent from the inverted index. When the node cannot fetch historical PairCreated events back to genesis, pools are fetched via factory_contract.allPairs.map() and appended to local variable 'initial_pools', but _pool_index builds from pools_obj._objects[:initial_count] — not initial_pools. Those pools appear in the 'Loaded N pools' log but are never added to the index. Fix: merge fallback pools into pools_obj._objects (before slicing for initial_count), or build the initial index from initial_pools instead of _objects.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated V2 Pool Loading Pipeline section to reflect v2-pool-index changes: _pool_index cached property, continuous discovery via is_reusable=True and non-cancelled events task, removal of get_pools_via_factory_getpair(), removal of ram_cache from all_pools_for(), O(1) index lookup. Added polling interval (30s) and _objects private API usage note.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update the mypy invocation in the skill to use the correct venv path for this project: /Users/bryan/code/ypricemagic-server/.venv/bin/python -m mypy y/ (not /Users/bryan/code/ypricemagic/.venv/bin/python). The wrong venv is referenced in the skill, causing workers to use the wrong Python environment.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Two separate workers (v2-price-regression-tests, v2-continuous-discovery) noted the skill references /Users/bryan/code/ypricemagic/.venv but the correct venv is /Users/bryan/code/ypricemagic-server/.venv. Both flagged this in skillFeedback.suggestedChanges.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Refine the 'Do NOT run tests locally' instruction to distinguish: (1) Structural tests (no-RPC, no brownie network) CAN be run locally on Mac — workers should run these; (2) Integration tests (those marked @mainnet_only or @pytest.mark.slow) cannot run locally and should be written for CI only. The current blanket prohibition causes workers to skip structural test verification unnecessarily.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: v2-continuous-discovery worker was able to run structural pytest tests locally (3 passing) by filtering with '-k not slow -p no:pytest_ethereum'. The skill's blanket prohibition caused initial confusion. Noted in sharedStateObservations of v2-continuous-discovery review.
